task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI application is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is clearly visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the synthetic data stream is actively running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: EEG waveforms are visible and rendering in the GUI, and the system shows signs of an active session (e.g., a 'Stop Data Stream' button is visible, indicating it is currently running).

task_completion.description: Verify the Time Series widget is displayed in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A panel or widget explicitly labeled 'Time Series' is visible within the OpenBCI GUI layout.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the Notch Filter settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The hardware settings, channel controller, or specific dropdown menu containing the Notch Filter options is opened or visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the Notch Filter is successfully changed to 50 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Notch Filter indicator or dropdown explicitly displays '50 Hz' (or '50Hz') within the Time Series widget.

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., using scrot, gnome-screenshot, or import) executing a save to '/tmp/notch_filter_result.png', or a GUI save dialog confirming this exact file path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI to change the setting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse clicks or interactions within the OpenBCI GUI are visible, rather than the agent using terminal commands to silently edit underlying configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify a real screenshot was captured instead of a dummy file being created.
integrity.visual_evidence: A genuine screenshot utility is used with the correct path, rather than commands like 'touch /tmp/notch_filter_result.png' or echoing text into a file.

integrity.description: Verify the results come from the genuine OpenBCI software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI appears authentic and is not a mockup opened in an image editor, web browser, or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Notch Filter setting is located within the 'Time Series' widget of the OpenBCI GUI. The expected value for the Notch Filter is exactly '50 Hz'. The final screenshot must be saved to the exact path '/tmp/notch_filter_result.png'.
